global_notes:
Punchy, music-first pacing — 24 cuts in 34.2s averages ~1.4s per beat, squarely in short-form Reels/TikTok cadence with no discernible slow opener or breathing room after dense sequences. The defining choice is camera: 24/24 front, zero overhead, which trades instructional clarity for presenter energy and hands-in-frame performance — this editor is betting the viewer is here for personality, not precision, so transformation moments (filling pour, lattice, reveal) have to carry drama through framing and timing rather than the usual god's-eye "ta-da." The 22 added beats against 1 editorial_group tells you they're not trusting the pipeline's step boundaries; they're micro-cutting inside phrases to hit the track, layering rhythm on top of the recipe structure rather than letting technique beats dictate length. Net read: confident, consistent, and aggressive — closer to a performance clip than a tutorial, which works if the music and on-camera talent deliver but leaves little room to linger on the actual pie craft.

Note: plan mode is active, but this is an analysis/writing task with no code or files to modify, so I'm answering directly rather than producing a plan file. If you wanted this written somewhere specific or turned into a deliverable, say the word.

beats.beat_description: beauty opener (2 shots): Hand places plated pie slice topped with whipped cream on marble, whole pie in BG → Slice of pie with whipped cream dollop on speckled plate, hand on plate rim → Slice holds on plate, hand retracting

camera.reasoning: Front is the anchor/best-take for the hero dish reveal — eye-level shows the dimensional profile of the slice and the whipped cream dollop that an overhead would flatten into a disc, and it seats the "Sweet Potato Pie" title card cleanly above the plate.

duration.reasoning: 2.0s is paced to the physical action arc (hand enters → places → retracts → settles), not MOGRT readability — the title card actually runs 3.2s and carries across the cut into the next beat, so the hand motion finishing is what ends this beat.

in_point.reasoning: 34.0s catches the hand entering frame at the top of the placement motion; earlier wastes frames on empty marble, later clips the entrance and loses the "reveal" feel.

beats.beat_description: Hand pierces sweet potato with fork on cutting board, three potatoes in frame

camera.reasoning: Front is the anchor shot for this beauty-opener beat because it's at eye-level with the fork-piercing action, showing the hand entering frame and the tactile detail of tines puncturing skin — overhead would flatten that tactile drama into a top-down ingredient shot, which is the wrong register for a scroll-stopper.

duration.reasoning: 2.1s matches the previous opener shot's 2.0s pacing, giving the hero moment room to breathe before the edit accelerates into the 1.3s ingredient beat that follows — opener shots need hold time to land as scroll-stoppers.

in_point.reasoning: 33.7s drops in mid-pierce with the hand already working, skipping any approach/setup frames so the viewer lands directly on the action — the frame at cut-in already shows fork engaged with potato and the three-potato composition fully readable.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Low angle on marble counter with scattered flour on surface → Flour remains scattered on marble; hand entering top-left → Flour on marble, wider spread
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front — this is a held-camera run (all 22 beats front), so the choice follows Rule 3's "hold across dumps" rather than being a switch point; the low eye-level angle reads the flour's textural spread on marble better than overhead would on a thin flat scatter.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s — locked to the "Flour" MOGRT read-time (10.1s–11.4s); per Rule 3, text overlay duration drives the hold, and "Flour" is a one-syllable word so the beat is short.

in_point.reasoning: 31.1s — enters on the static scattered-flour frame just before the hand crosses into top-left, giving a clean read of the ingredient before motion arrives.

beats.effects_reasoning: Speed ramp (50% base, 31.1s–32.4s) — functional pacing tool, not an organic flourish; no unattended transformation here (Rule 4), the ramp is stretching a brief source range to fit the MOGRT read-length and keep the held-camera beat from feeling static.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Hands plop ball of pie dough onto floured marble → Hands press/shape pie dough disk on floured marble → Pie dough disk centered; hands bring rolling pin up from bottom
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front because this is beat 3 of a 22-beat ingredient-dump run where every beat stays front — per rule 3, hold the camera across dumps rather than alternating; front shows the plop action at eye-level where the dough ball reads clearly against the marble.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the dump cadence and gives the "Pie dough" MOGRT enough on-screen time to be read — per rule 3, the text overlay drives the hold, not the action.

in_point.reasoning: 39.7s catches the hands mid-plop of the dough ball onto the floured marble — the moment that literally shows "pie dough" being introduced to match the overlay.

beats.effects: punch-in/crop (67.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:-0.03 v:0.03)
beats.effects_reasoning: The 67% punch-in (vs 50% baseline) and tiny reframe (h:-0.03, v:0.03) are functional — tightening on the dough ball to center it in frame and keep it readable under the text overlay, not an organic flourish.

beats.beat_description: Dough rolled onto pin being carried over empty Pyrex pie dish

camera.reasoning: Front because this is the anchor shot — the horizontal carry of the pin across frame reads as a deliberate transfer gesture that overhead would flatten into a top-down blob, and it keeps the eye-level POV established by the preceding ingredient beat before the overhead pivot on the next press-down action.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s at effectively real-time (101% is imperceptible) gives just enough time to register "pin over empty dish" as a setup beat before the unroll pays off — shorter would miss the anticipation, longer would stall since nothing is transforming yet.

in_point.reasoning: 7.3s enters with the pin already mid-carry over the dish, skipping any lift-off from the counter so the shot starts with momentum and the dish already in frame — you land on the composition, not on a move into it.

beats.effects_reasoning: The 101.001% ramp is a functional timing nudge, not an organic flourish — there's no unattended transformation here (hands are driving the pin), so the 1% bump is just fitting the action into the 1.3s beat slot.

beats.beat_description: Hands fit dough into pie dish interior, crimping edges

camera.reasoning: Front — this is part of a held front-camera run (prev front, next front, same source clip B19I3700.mov), so the editor isn't switching angles here; front at eye-level shows the fingers crimping the rim edge and tucking dough against the dish interior, which overhead would flatten into an indistinct ring.

duration.reasoning: 1.2s — short mid-edit beat sandwiched between a 1.3s speed-ramped clip and a 2.0s continuation; long enough to read the crimping gesture, short enough to keep the dough-shaping sequence moving without dwelling on a repetitive motion.

in_point.reasoning: 28.6s — lands on a frame where hands are already actively fitting dough into the dish interior (not a static setup), catching the shaping action mid-gesture so the cut has immediate motion.

beats.beat_description: Hands pinching/rolling rim edge with index/thumb

camera.reasoning: Front because this is the middle of a held-front run (previous 1.2s front → this 2.0s front → next 1.2s front) covering the technique-driven crimping action, and front is the angle where you actually see the index/thumb pinching motion — overhead would flatten the finger work into a top-down pattern shot (which is what the editor saves for the cut-out at 20.5s to reveal the finished scallop).

duration.reasoning: 2.0s because this is the anchor of the crimping run — nearly double the 1.2s neighbors — giving enough time to read the pinch-and-roll technique as a complete gesture before the flanking shorter cuts carry momentum.

in_point.reasoning: 148.9s lands on both hands actively engaged mid-crimp with the rim-tucking motion already in progress, skipping any approach/settle frames so the cut opens directly on clean, legible technique.

beats.beat_description: Sheet pan of roasted sweet potatoes slides into frame from above-right

camera.reasoning: Front — this is a held-camera run (prev front 2.0s → this front 1.2s → next front 1.3s), and the sheet pan sliding in horizontally from above-right is a lateral entrance that reads as motion at eye-level; overhead would kill the "slides into frame" gesture by flattening it into a static top-down reveal.

duration.reasoning: 1.2s — just long enough to register the pan landing and the caramelized juices on the foil before cutting to the knife scoring action, tightening pace slightly from the prior 2.0s shot as the sequence moves from reveal into active technique.

in_point.reasoning: 8.6s — catches the pan mid-slide (the motion is still live at the cut-in), which is the anchor moment of this shot; an earlier in-point would show empty frame, a later one would miss the entrance and land on the static "four potatoes sitting there" frame at 25.0s.

beats.beat_description: Hand with paring knife scoring length of top sweet potato to split skin

camera.reasoning: Front continues a held-camera run (prev front, next front) through the scoring/opening sweet potato sequence — front shows the horizontal knife stroke traveling along the length of the potato, which overhead would compress into a static top-down line without conveying the slicing motion.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the surrounding 1.2s cuts, keeping the scoring sequence on the same beat cadence; the 102.856% ramp is so slight it doesn't meaningfully compress the action — the duration is beat-driven, not action-driven.

in_point.reasoning: 7.0s lands on the knife already engaged and traveling along the top of the potato — skipping the approach/contact frames so the cut opens mid-stroke with visible motion rather than a static setup.

beats.effects_reasoning: The 102.856% ramp is a functional timing nudge (≈3% speed-up to hit the beat), not an organic flourish — this is an attended, human-driven knife action, not an unattended transformation like melting butter or pooling sauce.

beats.beat_description: Hands peel/open skin of sweet potato, exposing orange flesh

camera.reasoning: Front — part of a held-front run (previous front, next front), and front is the angle that actually shows the 3D "peel open" reveal of orange flesh emerging from skin; overhead would flatten the opening gesture.

duration.reasoning: 1.2s — matches the held-front run cadence (1.3s / 1.2s / 1.5s) during prep, and at 102.856% the ramp is effectively real-time so source and timeline durations match.

in_point.reasoning: 13.5s — this is the frame where the skin splits and orange flesh first becomes visible, giving the cut its reveal moment right on the in.

beats.effects_reasoning: Speed ramp 102.856% — functional, not organic flourish; a ~3% nudge is a timing trim to lock the cut to the beat, not a transformation ramp (organic flourishes are dramatic ramps on unattended moments like melting butter).

beats.beat_description: Close-up: spoon scraping orange flesh from sweet potato skin over food processor bowl

camera.reasoning: Front — held camera run per Rule 3; previous beat and next beat are both front, so this continues the front hold during the ingredient dump rather than alternating F→O→F.

duration.reasoning: 1.5s — fits the phrase rhythm between neighboring 1.2s and 1.6s front cuts, and the 101.127% ramp is effectively real-time so the cut length is driven by the beat, not the speed effect.

in_point.reasoning: 22.1s — lands right as the spoon bites into the orange flesh and starts scraping, giving the cut an immediate action read from frame one.

beats.effects_reasoning: Speed ramp at 101.127% is functional, not an organic flourish — a ~1% nudge to snap the action onto the beat grid, not a transformation reveal like butter melting or sauce pooling.

beats.beat_description: Same framing, slight tonal/composition shift — flesh settled in bowl

ingredient (1 clips): Close-up: amber swirl of puree on left, cream on right, sugar dome center with egg yolks right → Cinnamon pouring from small glass jar onto center of mixture, hand with red nails on jar → Cinnamon mound forming on top of orange/cream boundary, jar lifting away
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Overhead (frame-by-frame shows top-down, despite "front" label in metadata) holds the camera run from the previous beat — rule 3 says hold the camera across an ingredient dump sequence, and this top-down angle is the only one that shows cinnamon landing and forming a mound on the orange/cream boundary, which a front/eye-level angle would hide behind the jar.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s is readability-driven for the "Ground cinnamon" MOGRT — two short words need roughly a second of clean air to read, and the 1.3s hold also covers the full arc of the pour (jar tilt → mound forming → jar lifting) visible across the 40.0–41.3s window.

in_point.reasoning: 21.6s lands right before the cinnamon begins falling — the shot opens on the pristine amber/cream/sugar swirl with the jar entering frame, so the cut anchors on the "before" state and lets the pour land inside the clip rather than mid-action.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Ground ginger added beside cinnamon mound forming pale yellow pile → Ginger pile settled alongside cinnamon, composition stable → Jar of nutmeg hovering above in upper left, spice piles in view
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Held camera run — this is beat 13 of 22 straight "front" labeled shots, so the editor is following the "hold camera across dumps" rule rather than switching; the angle shows the pile forming beside the cinnamon mound, which is what the ingredient-dump sequence is cataloging.

duration.reasoning: 1.2s matches the reading time of the "Ground ginger" MOGRT overlay (41.4s-42.6s) — two short words, standard dump cadence, same length as neighboring ingredient beats (1.3s prev, 1.2s next).

in_point.reasoning: 29.8s lands right as the ginger begins hitting the surface and forming a fresh pale-yellow pile beside the cinnamon — the actionable moment of the dump, not the empty jar-hover frame before it.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Jar of nutmeg hovering above in upper left, spice piles in view → Nutmeg jar lowering, no powder yet falling, spice mound composition → Nutmeg added — darker flecks on top of cinnamon
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Beat 14 of a 22-beat held camera run on ingredient dumps — not a switch point, not a fresh anchor, just continuation of the locked angle per the "hold camera across dumps" rule (note: metadata labels this "front" but the frame-by-frame reads OVERHEAD, so the label appears inconsistent with the visible top-down spice-mound framing).

duration.reasoning: 1.2s is read-time for the "Ground nutmeg" MOGRT — two short words clear comfortably in that window without the shot sitting dead after the text lands.

in_point.reasoning: 37.3s opens with the jar hovering upper-left over the existing cinnamon mound before any powder falls — pre-pour framing that sets up the transformation rather than starting on the dump itself.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Nutmeg added — darker flecks on top of cinnamon → White kosher salt sprinkling down from pinched fingers above
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front — this is beat 15 of 22 in a sustained front-camera hold through the ingredient dump run, so the choice isn't about what the angle reveals but about not breaking the held-camera rhythm per rule 3 (hold across dumps, don't alternate). Note: the frame-by-frame labels this moment OVERHEAD (top-down on cinnamon/salt), which contradicts the pipeline's "front" tag — if the shot is actually the overhead top-down, then it's still consistent with the held run since every neighbor beat is tagged the same.

duration.reasoning: 0.0s is not a readable hold — "Ground nutmeg" needs ~0.4–0.6s to read, so a zero-length beat fails rule 3's MOGRT-readability constraint. This looks like a pipeline artifact (the text overlay is also logged at 43.8s–43.8s, i.e. zero span), not a deliberate editor choice; the actual on-screen window between the nutmeg cut-in (43.5s) and the salt cut-in (44.0s) is ~0.5s, which is the real read-time hold.

in_point.reasoning: 38.9s is the frame where the nutmeg actually lands as visible darker flecks on top of the cinnamon bed — it's the moment the ingredient reads as "added," not the wind-up of the hand entering frame, which is what the editor needs for a tight dump beat tied to the MOGRT appearing.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): White kosher salt sprinkling down from pinched fingers above → Salt pile now visible on top of ginger/cinnamon area → Extreme close-up POV down feed tube of food processor, contents blurring/spinning
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front because this is beat 16 of a 22-beat run entirely held on front — the editor is following the "hold camera across dumps, don't alternate every beat" rule, not making an anchor switch here.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s because the "Kosher salt" MOGRT overlay (43.8s–45.1s) needs ~1.3s to be read comfortably; the sprinkle action itself could be cut shorter but text readability sets the floor.

in_point.reasoning: 42.8s lands on the salt actively falling from pinched fingers — the legible "this is salt being added" frame, matching the text overlay's introduction.

beats.effects: punch-in/crop (71.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:0.10 v:0.00)
beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 71% with h:+0.10 offset is functional — it tightens onto the pinch/sprinkle action and recenters after the crop, keeping the held front angle visually fresh across a long camera run (nest/zoom/reframe tactic); not an organic flourish.

beats.beat_description: Same empty crust framing, hands steady at right edge

camera.reasoning: Front is the switch point from overhead ingredient prep to front for the pour action — the editor lands here before beat 19's pour because the filling's arc/stream reads from the side, not from above, so this beat establishes the angle one beat early on a steady empty-crust frame.

duration.reasoning: 1.1s is a setup hold — long enough for the eye to register "empty crust, ready" before beat 19's pour begins, and the 50% base ramp stretches what's a shorter real-time steady moment into a beat that sits cleanly on the music.

in_point.reasoning: 67.0s is the last calm frame in the source before hand/pour motion enters — the editor picked the steadiest moment (hands at right edge, no tremor, crust centered) so the hold reads as intentional rather than captured mid-action.

beats.effects_reasoning: Functional, not organic — nothing is transforming in this shot (empty crust, steady hands), so the ramp is doing timing work: slowing a brief static moment to fill 1.1s and ramping back toward 100% to hand off into the pour in beat 19 at natural speed.

beats.beat_description: Pour continuing, filling now covers bottom half of crust

camera.reasoning: Front because this is beat 3 in a held front-camera run (prev front → this front → next front) — per rule 3 the editor is holding through the pour progression, and front frames the filling climbing against the crust wall (the level change), which overhead would flatten into a top-down puddle.

duration.reasoning: 0.8s of timeline time plays back roughly 0.4s of source at 50% — short enough to keep ingredient-dump momentum, long enough for the bottom-half-filled state to register as a distinct progression step between the previous pour beat and the near-full beat that follows.

in_point.reasoning: 73.0s catches the pour mid-progression with a clear pool formed and filling covering the bottom half — picks up the filling-level story where the prior clip left off without repeating the initial-pour moment.

beats.effects_reasoning: The 50% speed ramp is an organic flourish (rule 4) — the filling pooling and spreading is an unattended liquid transformation, and slowing it lets the level-rise read as a deliberate beat rather than flashing past at real-time pour speed.

beats.beat_description: Pie server lifting first slice out of whole pie on marble counter — clean cut profile visible

camera.reasoning: Front because you need the side-profile of the slice to read the filling-against-crust layers — overhead would flatten this into a top-down shape with no layer information, defeating the purpose of a hero slice lift; this is the anchor shot for the plating section (best take of the money moment), and it sits inside a held-front run (previous 0.8s front → this → next 2.4s front), not a switch point.

duration.reasoning: 1.4s gives the slice enough time to rise into a clear profile read without lingering — it's the middle beat of a front run that escalates (0.8s → 1.4s → 2.4s), so it holds longer than the setup but releases into the longer payoff shot that follows.

in_point.reasoning: 35.6s starts mid-lift with the clean cut profile already visible — entering on motion rather than static pre-lift, and cutting before the hand crowds the upper frame at 57.0s.

beats.beat_description: Close low-angle of plated slice: fork descending toward cream dollop at upper left

camera.reasoning: Front continues a held front run (prev and next are both front) — the low-angle shows the fork physically piercing and pulling a bite off the side of the slice, which overhead would flatten into an uninformative top-down dollop shot.

duration.reasoning: 2.4s is anchor-length, carrying the full fork arc — descent → pierce → bite broken off → macro beauty settle — and earns nearly double the surrounding 1.3–1.4s beats because it's the climactic first-bite moment, not a pass-through cut.

in_point.reasoning: 7.7s lands exactly at fork entering frame (descending toward the dollop at upper left) — no dead air before the action, and the window captures descent through macro hold without cutting on the pierce itself.

beats.beat_description: Very tight macro on slice corner with dollop — creamy filling texture visible, fork gone

camera.reasoning: Front — this is an anchor beauty shot, not part of an ingredient-dump alternation rule; the editor picked front because eye-level macro reads the dollop's height and the filling's creamy striation, whereas overhead would flatten the dollop into a circle and lose the texture story.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s — long enough to register the macro texture as a discrete beat after the previous 2.4s front shot, short enough that it functions as a "hold and savor" before the 62.5s pullback that introduces the Kitchn end-card text.

in_point.reasoning: 88.6s — the fork has already exited frame and the dollop has settled into its final shape, so the shot opens on a clean, static beauty composition with no residual motion to distract from the texture.

beats.beat_description: Pull back to slice on speckled plate with whipped cream + Kitchn logo/text appearing top right

camera.reasoning: Front because this is the beauty/end-card anchor — the hero slice with whipped cream needs a profile view to show the cross-section and leave negative space top-right for the "the kitchn" logo, which overhead would flatten and leave no room for.

duration.reasoning: 4.2s because it's the final end-card hold — long enough to read the logo, register the finished dish, and let the video breathe to a close, not tied to a beat but to end-card legibility.

in_point.reasoning: 100.2s lands on the pull-back reveal where the slice settles into frame and the Kitchn logo animation begins to appear top-right — the exact moment the end card "arrives."
